title: Generation of processor interrupts using averaged data
abstract: In an embodiment, a processor includes at least one execution unit to execute instructions, and an interrupt generation unit. The interrupt generation unit may be to: receive a plurality of values indicating thermal status values for a memory unit at multiple points in time across a first time window; determine a running average value based on the plurality of values indicating thermal status values in the memory unit; and in response to a determination that the running average value has exceeded a high thermal status threshold value, generate a thermal interrupt indicating a high thermal status event in the processor. Other embodiments are described and claimed.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A processor comprising: at least one execution unit to execute instructions; and an interrupt generation unit to: receive a plurality of values indicating thermal status values for a memory unit at multiple points in time across a time window; determine a running average value based on the plurality of values indicating thermal status values in the memory unit; and in response to a determination that the running average value has exceeded a high thermal status threshold value, generate a thermal interrupt indicating a high thermal status event in the processor. 2. The processor of claim 1, wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: access a high threshold register to read the high thermal status threshold value; and compare the running average value to the high thermal status threshold value. 3. The processor of claim 2, wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: access a low threshold register to read a low thermal status value; and compare the running average value to the low thermal status threshold value. 4. The processor of claim 3, wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: in response to a determination that the running average value has dropped below the low thermal status threshold value, generate a second thermal interrupt indicating a low thermal status event in the processor. 5. The processor of claim 1, wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: access a time window register to read data describing a length of the time window, wherein the length of the time window is set based on thermal characteristics of the memory unit. 6. The processor of claim 1, wherein the time window is a first time window, and wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: 

 receive a set of values indicating power consumption levels in the processor at multiple points in time across a second time window; determine a second running average value based on the set of values indicating power consumption levels in the processor; and in response to a determination that the second running average value has exceeded a high power consumption threshold, generate a power interrupt indicating a high power consumption event in the processor. 7. The processor of claim 6, wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: in response to a determination that the second running average value has dropped below a low power threshold value, generate a thermal interrupt indicating a low power consumption event in the processor. 8. The processor of claim 1, wherein the interrupt generation unit is further to: read, at different points in time, the plurality of values indicating thermal status values from a thermal status register included in the memory unit. 9. A method comprising: receiving a plurality of values indicating thermal status levels for a memory unit at multiple points in time across a time window; determining a running average value based on the plurality of values indicating thermal status levels in the memory unit; and in response to a determination that the running average value has reached a first threshold value, generating a first thermal interrupt indicating a thermal status event in the memory unit. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein determining the running average value comprises using one selected from a simple running average algorithm, a weighted running average algorithm, an exponential running average algorithm, and a leaky bucket running average algorithm. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the plurality of values comprises thermal status levels read from a status register included in the memory unit. 12. The method of claim 9, further comprising: accessing a high threshold register to read the first threshold value, wherein the thermal interrupt indicates a high thermal status event in the memory unit. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: accessing a low threshold register to read a second threshold value; comparing the running average value to the second threshold value; and in response to a determination that the running average value has dropped below the second threshold value, generate a second thermal interrupt indicating a low thermal status event in the memory unit. 14. The method of claim 9, further comprising: accessing a time window register to access information describing the time window. 15. A machine readable medium having stored thereon data, which if used by at least one machine, causes the at least one machine to fabricate at least one integrated circuit to perform a method according to any one of claims 9 to 14. 16. An apparatus for processing instructions, configured to perform the method of any one of claims 9 to 14. 17. A system comprising: a processor comprising interrupt generation circuitry to: receive a plurality of values indicating power consumption levels in the processor at multiple points in time across a time window; determine a running average value based on the plurality of values indicating power consumption levels in the processor; and 

 in response to a determination that the running average value has reached a first power threshold value, generate a first power interrupt indicating a first power event in the processor; and a system memory coupled to the processor. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the interrupt generation circuitry is further to: access a low threshold register to determine the first power threshold value; and compare the running average value to the first power threshold value, the first power interrupt to indicate a low power consumption event in the processor. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the time window is a first time window, and wherein the interrupt generation circuitry is further to: receive a set of values indicating power consumption levels in the processor at multiple points in time across a second time window; determine a second running average value based on the set of values indicating power consumption levels in the processor across the second time window; access a high threshold register to determine a second power threshold value; and in response to a determination that the second running average value has exceeded the second power threshold value, generate a second power interrupt to indicate a high power consumption event in the processor. 20. The system of claim 17, wherein the interrupt generation circuitry is further to: receive a set of values indicating thermal status levels for a memory unit at multiple points in time across a third time window; determine a third running average value based on the set of values indicating thermal status levels in the memory unit; and in response to a determination that the third running average value has exceeded a high thermal status threshold value, generate a third power interrupt to indicate a high thermal status event in the memory unit. 21. The system of claim 17, wherein the interrupt generation circuitry is further to: access a time window register to read data describing a length of the time window, wherein the length of the time window is determined based on power characteristics of the processor. 22. The system of claim 17, wherein the interrupt generation circuitry is to determine the running average value using one selected from a simple running average algorithm, a weighted running average algorithm, an exponential running average algorithm, and a leaky bucket running average algorithm.
